events.title: Freedom Fields Festival
events.body: From midday at The Farm Animal Sanctuary, Evesham (runs across the weekend).

events.title: Badsey Flower Show
events.body: One of the area's oldest village shows returns for its 123rd year, from 1pm at Badsey.

events.title: Flat Tonic — live music at the Club
Illyria: The Pirates of Penzance — Lenches Amphitheatre
events.body: The award-winning Illyria return to the open-air stage. Performance 4pm, gates 2.30pm, with a pop-up café, ice creams and licensed bar.

events.title: Messy Church — Church Lench
events.body: An informal, craft-and-activity based afternoon for families, 3.30–5.30pm. Regular Sunday services that morning at Rous Lench (8.30am), Harvington (10am) and Church Lench (11am).

events.title: Battle of Evesham Festival — Crown & Corporation Meadow
events.body: Europe's largest 13th-century battle re-enactment, running into the weekend. The Friday evening opens with a Queen tribute act, and the talks programme includes Alice Roberts and the Medieval Baebes on the Saturday.

events.title: Community coffee morning — Abbots Morton Village Hall
events.body: Drop in for a coffee and a chat, 10am to midday.

events.title: Cash Bingo — The Lenches Club
events.body: The Club's monthly bingo night returns. Over-18s only.

events.title: 'Bimble' car drive-out — Abbots Morton
events.body: An afternoon run out for classic and interesting cars, setting off from Abbots Morton from 12.45pm.

events.title: Glo-Quiz — The Lenches Club
events.body: The Club's glow-in-the-dark quiz night, from 8pm.

events.title: Sounds of the 60s: The Nightingales — Lenches Amphitheatre
Mikron Theatre: Top of the Wold — Evesham
events.body: A touring company's new play about a mobile library, celebrating community and culture. From 2.30pm.

events.title: Evesham Scarecrow Festival — Hampton Ferry
events.body: Scarecrow-making, live music, stalls and the Rotary duck race, 10am–5pm.

events.title: Murder Mystery Night: Bingo! Bonkers! — The Vale Golf Club
events.body: A bingo night with a sinister twist, from 7pm.

events.title: Jeeves and Wooster: Stiff Upper Lip — Lenches Amphitheatre
events.body: Alfresco Actors perform Wodehouse's comedy. Gates 1pm, performance 2.30–4.40pm.

eventsFootnote: Weekly regulars: yoga at Abbots Morton Village Hall on Mondays (9.45am), Lenches Tennis Club cardio on Tuesdays (6.30pm), tai chi at Abbots Morton on Wednesdays (10am), and candlelight yoga at Rous Lench Village Hall on Thursdays (6.30pm). Further ahead at the Club: sports bar closed 13–14 Aug, a private wedding reception 15 Aug, Cash Bingo 21 Aug and a private function 22 Aug.

news.title: Six weeks of roadworks at Pinvin
news.body: Main Street in Pinvin closes from today until 28 August while the gas mains are upgraded — worth allowing extra time if you head that way towards Pershore or Worcester.

news.title: Cricket club: new players welcome
news.body: Players of all ages and abilities are wanted, whether experienced, returning or brand new. Contact Venky on 07442 772470 or Charlie on 07775 660065.

news.title: Summer of Heritage across Worcestershire
news.body: Wychavon's summer heritage programme is bringing history events across the county, including this month's Battle of Evesham Festival.

notices.title: Car stolen at Offenham
notices.body: A white Audi was taken from a driveway in Offenham this weekend. A good moment to check keys are stored away from the front door and that vehicles are locked overnight.

notices.title: ARCH Messenger deadline
notices.body: Copy for the next edition of the benefice magazine is due by Monday 10 August.

notices.title: Newsletter deadline
notices.body: Got something for next week? Send it to website@thelenches.org.uk by the end of Wednesday.
